### Post by sandyd on 2009-03-20
Im having compositing problems on my comp

when I have
```

Section "Extensions"
Option "Composite" "True"
EndSection

```
the applications show up like this (see screenshot attachment) look at the icons in the tray....

When I have 
```

Section "Extensions"
Option "Composite" "false"
EndSection

```

there are no problems whatsoever, but no effects...

help me............

xorg.conf
```

# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildd@palmer)  Mon Nov  3 08:46:46 UTC 2008

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)

# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using

# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated

#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Default Layout"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "glx"
#	Disable "dri2"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver         "kbd"
    Option         "XkbRules" "xorg"
    Option         "XkbModel" "pc105"
    Option         "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Configured Mouse"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Synaptics Touchpad"
    Driver         "synaptics"
    Option         "SendCoreEvents" "true"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto-dev"
    Option         "HorizEdgeScroll" "0"
EndSection


Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "LG L1919S"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 83.0
    VertRefresh     56.0 - 75.0
EndSection



Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce FX 5500"
    Option         "RenderAccel" "true"
    Option         "AllowGLXWithComposite" "true"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "0"
    Option         "TwinViewXineramaInfoOrder" "CRT-0"
    Option         "metamodes" "1280x1024_75 +0+0; nvidia-auto-select +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection
Section "Extensions"
 Option "Composite" "False"
 EndSection
```

### Post by Silent Warrior on 2009-03-20
Well - first of all, I know next to nothing about the inner workings of KDE 4.2 (haven't got to try it yet), and I don't really know what's the deal with composite graphics either. So, you should definitely not act on my advice alone.
BUT... I seem to remember reading a blog somewhere about Xinerama (or something with a similar name) didn't support something or other that had to do with Compiz. All I can suggest is that you search for Compiz-trouble, and I think someone has a link to the blog where this is explained (or a link to a blog with a link to The Blog, see?) in their signature. Nope, I don't know the address there off the top of my head, and I doubt I'd have more success in finding it than you would...
